name: Marinated Eggplant
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the eggplant into slices about 3/4-inch thick.
2. Arrange the slices on a dishtowel and sprinkle with salt. Let rest for about 10 minutes to allow the water to leach out. Wipe dry and set aside.
3. Beat eggs with parsley, a pinch of salt, and pepper in a bowl.
4. Heat 2 cups olive oil in a deep skillet.
5. Flour eggplant slices lightly, then dip in egg wash. Saute each side for about 1 minute until golden brown, then place on an absorbent towel to cool.
6. In a heavy saucepan with remaining oil, saute garlic until golden.
7. Remove from heat, add vinegar, and return to heat. Add bay leaves, rosemary (if using), and pepper to taste, simmering for 1 minute.
8. Assemble eggplant layers in a bowl with a lid, sprinkling liberally with marinade between each layer. Pour remaining marinade over the dish.
9. Cover and turn upside down to mix in the marinade. Let stand at room temperature for 2 hours (up to 2 days refrigerated or up to 2 weeks frozen).
10. Unmold onto a serving plate and serve at room temperature.
